title: VPN for Streaming in 2026: Which VPNs Still Work with Netflix, BBC iPlayer, and Disney+
description: Streaming services actively block VPN IP addresses. Most VPNs fail within weeks of working. This guide covers which VPNs reliably unblock major streaming platforms and why others fail.
content:
Why Streaming and VPNs Are in a Constant Arms Race

Streaming services license content by territory. A show available in the UK may not be licensed for the US, and vice versa. When you use a VPN to appear to be in a different country, you access content the streaming platform is not licensed to show you. The platforms detect and block VPN servers to enforce these licensing agreements.

The detection works primarily by identifying datacenter IP addresses. When you connect to a VPN server, you get an IP address that belongs to a datacenter (AWS, Google Cloud, Hetzner, etc.) rather than a residential ISP. Streaming services maintain lists of known datacenter IP ranges and block them. VPN providers counter this by acquiring residential IP addresses or rotating their datacenter IPs frequently.

Which VPNs Actually Work in 2026

ExpressVPN remains the most consistent for streaming. Their Lightway protocol has lower latency than competitors, and they maintain a large enough server fleet to rotate IPs when one set gets blocked. Netflix, BBC iPlayer, Disney+, Hulu, and Amazon Prime Video all work reliably. Expensive at $10-13/month.

NordVPN works well for Netflix and Disney+ and has specific streaming-optimized servers. BBC iPlayer unblocking is less consistent than ExpressVPN. The SmartPlay DNS feature handles some streaming without the full VPN tunnel, reducing buffering.

Surfshark offers good streaming performance at a lower price point. Their unlimited device connection policy is useful for households with many devices. Netflix and Disney+ work reliably; BBC iPlayer is hit or miss.

Proton VPN works for Netflix but less consistently for BBC iPlayer and regional services. Better suited for privacy-focused users than streaming-focused ones.

Why Free VPNs Fail for Streaming

Free VPNs use smaller server fleets with shared IP addresses. These IPs get detected and blocked rapidly. Most free VPNs have not maintained functional Netflix or iPlayer unblocking for years. If streaming access is your primary use case, a paid subscription is the only option that works reliably.

Troubleshooting When It Stops Working

VPN IPs get blocked frequently, sometimes daily. The first step when a stream stops working is to switch to a different server in the same country. Most providers have multiple servers per country. If switching servers does not work, clear your browser cookies (the streaming service may have stored your previous session's location data) and try again. If the problem persists, contact your VPN's support to ask which servers are currently working for that specific service.
